verdicts.title: Potential Local File Inclusion via tools.php ?tool= parameter
verdicts.details: The taint analysis flagged `require_once WPSEO_PATH . 'admin/views/tool-' . $tool_page . '.php'` as LFI. However the code immediately preceding it (lines 79-87 of tools.php) validates $tool_page against a strict allowlist: `$tool_pages = ['bulk-editor', 'import-export', 'file-editor']`. The `in_array($tool_page, $tool_pages, true)` check with strict comparison prevents injection. Additionally, the file begins with `if (!defined('WPSEO_VERSION')) { exit(); }` ensuring it only loads from within WordPress. This is NOT exploitable.

verdicts.title: SQL Injection via meta_ids array used in DELETE - WPSEO_Meta::clean_up()
verdicts.details: The DELETE query at line 897 uses meta_ids fetched from $wpdb->get_col() in a prior SELECT query. The concern would only be valid if $meta_ids could contain attacker-controlled data. The meta_ids are integer primary keys returned by a server-side SELECT on the postmeta table; they are not influenced by user input. The taint analysis incorrectly traced database-origin data as tainted. This is an internal orphan cleanup method called only from WPSEO_Meta::clean_up(), which itself is called only from the private upgrade_15() routine in class-upgrade.php (line 179) during plugin upgrade - an admin-only operation. NOT exploitable.

verdicts.title: SQL Injection via orphan IDs array in indexable-cleanup-repository.php
verdicts.details: The DELETE statement at line 495 uses `$orphans` (integer IDs from a preceding SELECT). The SELECT query at line 475-484 is parameterized via $wpdb->prepare() and returns object_id values which are database integer primary keys. These are not influenced by user-controlled input. The cleanup methods are called exclusively from Cleanup_Integration::run_cleanup() via action hooks (wpseo_start_cleanup_indexables and wpseo_cleanup_cron) - internal scheduled/admin-triggered operations. NOT unauthenticated and NOT exploitable.

verdicts.title: SQL Injection via user orphan IDs in indexable-cleanup-repository.php
verdicts.details: Same pattern as sqli-002. $orphans are integer object_ids from a prepared SELECT. The DELETE only accepts these internal DB-derived integers. Called from admin cron/upgrade paths. NOT exploitable.

verdicts.title: SQL Injection via $orphans in generic cleanup delete - indexable-cleanup-repository.php
verdicts.details: Same as sqli-002/003. $orphans sourced from prepared SELECT, used in internal cron/admin cleanup. NOT exploitable.

verdicts.title: Object Injection via maybe_unserialize on options table value - class-upgrade.php
verdicts.details: The maybe_unserialize at line 1366 operates on data from $wpdb->options (a prepared SELECT). To exploit, an attacker would need to pre-poison the wp_options table with a malicious serialized payload, which requires database write access or admin-level access to inject options. The code runs only during plugin upgrade routines. NOT unauthenticated.

verdicts.title: REST API /yoast/v1/statistics requires 'read' capability (subscriber-level)
verdicts.details: The /yoast/v1/statistics endpoint uses `current_user_can('read')` as permission_callback, meaning any logged-in subscriber can access SEO statistics. The data exposed is aggregate post counts by SEO score. Information disclosure only - no PII or sensitive data. The /yoast/v1/file_size endpoint correctly requires 'manage_options'. Verified live against http://localhost:8880/wp-json/yoast/v1/statistics - returns 401 without auth.

verdicts.title: XSS flows in admin AJAX (xss-001, xss-002, xss-003) - authenticated admin context
verdicts.details: Flows wordpress-seo-xss-001/002/003 in admin/ajax.php are marked auth_required=true and nonce_verified=true by the scanner itself. The output is JSON-encoded via WPSEO_Utils::format_json_encode(), which provides safe encoding for JSON context. The sink (wp_die with JSON) is not a DOM XSS vector since a JSON response consumed by JavaScript does not inject into the DOM without explicit innerHTML assignment. The XSS label from the taint engine is a false classification - JSON output is not an HTML sink. Not exploitable.

unauth_rest_api_access_to_seo_metadata.notes: All /yoast/v1/* REST endpoints tested live - all require at minimum 'read' capability (authenticated user). No unauthenticated data disclosure confirmed.

sql_injection_in_sitemap_queries.notes: Sitemap query parameter flows (flow open-002) reach wp_safe_redirect which restricts to same-host URLs. The ?sitemap= query var feeds into home_url() construction and wp_safe_redirect() - open redirect limited to same origin. SQL injection in cleanup/upgrade SQL is not user-controlled.

stored_xss_via_meta_descriptions.notes: Meta description storage goes through WPSEO sanitization (sanitize_text_field, wp_strip_all_tags). Admin AJAX XSS flows are false positives as described. Unauth stored XSS path not found.

unauth_sitemap_manipulation.notes: Sitemap is generated and served from cached XML, not directly modifiable by unauthenticated requests.
